subject: Lance Armstrong
context: Public denial of doping allegations during the height of his Tour de France dominance.
ground_truth_source: Confessed in January 2013 interview with Oprah Winfrey; lifetime ban from cycling and stripped of seven Tour de France titles by USADA.

signals.transcript: I want to go back and ask you some questions now moving off the 2004 Tour de France. You attended the deposition of Ms. Betsy Andrew, did you not? Correct. And you heard her testimony regarding certain statements you were alleged to have made.

llm_report.behavioral_summary: Lance Armstrong's denial shows a heart-rate delta of 20.2 bpm and an F0 delta of 314.7 Hz, both elevated relative to a neutral baseline. AU15 lip-suppression peaked at 2.4; hedging language appeared 0 time(s) in the 163 wpm utterance. The composite pattern is consistent with patterns observed in historically resolved false denials.
llm_report.comparative_profile: Among archive entries with confirmed false-denial outcomes, this clip's deception score sits in the upper range. The pattern resembles cases later resolved against the speaker on the public record.
llm_report.qualifications: This is not a truth determination. Physiological arousal can reflect stress, fear, anger, fatigue, or other causes. Some signals on this clip were extracted using fallback estimators where the source video did not permit clean rPPG / AU extraction; treat the report as a research-grade prototype, not a forensic verdict.
